title: Mapping Housing and Food Insecurity in San Antonio
summary: A block-group-level map of Bexar County integrating five public datasets to find where housing cost burden and food access actually overlap.
detail: Integrated 5 public datasets — Census ACS, USDA Food Desert Atlas, USDA SNAP retailers, and food bank / WIC clinic locations — across ~1,139 Bexar County block groups.
Built a composite housing insecurity score from renter cost burden, severe cost burden (>50% of income on housing), and overcrowding.
Used a KD-tree spatial index to compute food-access distances at 1-mile and 0.5-mile thresholds.
Produced a 4-quadrant overlap map classifying every block group by housing and food-access status.
Automated 3 GitHub Actions pipelines — annual ACS/USDA refresh, monthly grocery-store re-pull, and continuous site deploy — keeping ongoing maintenance under 1 hour a year.
Shipped as a live interactive Leaflet.js map on GitHub Pages.

title: ITS Comparative Benchmarking
summary: Gap analysis of Trinity's IT services against 75 peer and aspirant universities, built for senior leadership.
detail: Cleaned and structured 35,000+ data points across 467 metrics from 75 universities using advanced Excel (XLOOKUP, PivotTables, Text-to-Columns) to ensure data integrity for benchmarking.
Ran gap analysis against 38 peer schools and 26 aspirant schools.
Built 12 Power BI visualizations presented to senior leadership.

title: Division Performance Projection — Trinity Swim & Dive
summary: Projected conference-transition placement for Trinity Swim & Dive using 2025 STAC Championship results, used directly by the coaching staff.
detail: Cleaned and analyzed 192 performance records from the 2025 STAC Championship in Excel.
Built 5 Tableau visualizations modeling competitive positioning against future conference opponents.
Gave the coaching staff actionable insights for recruitment and training focus during the SAA transition.

title: Institutional Research Publication Pipeline
summary: A data pipeline pulling and cleaning ~1.2 million works from the OpenAlex API to compare Trinity's research output against 99 peer and aspirant institutions.
detail: Built a data pipeline pulling publication records from the OpenAlex API across 99 peer and aspirant institutions into a dozen-table database of 1.2 million works.

Vibe-coded an interactive visualization app with Claude so university decision-makers can explore institution- and department-level publication rankings — applying Discernment to check generated code against the spec and Diligence to verify the underlying data before treating any output as reliable.

Caught a data-quality issue when early results showed implausible department rankings (Chemistry appearing as one of Trinity's lowest-publishing departments, which was untrue); traced it to OpenAlex's subject-classification tags inflating some departments and suppressing others.

Rebuilt department-level classification using Scopus journal rankings instead — classifying each work by the journal it was published in rather than OpenAlex's tags — to restore accurate department comparisons.
Presenting at Trinity's summer research symposium in late July 2026.

title: Peer Practice: AI Training Simulator for Peer Supporters
summary: A full-stack simulator where volunteer peer supporters practice conversations with an AI-played veteran and get structured, rubric-based feedback afterward.
detail: Built a full-stack training simulator with Next.js, TypeScript, and the Anthropic API — the AI plays the veteran seeking support, never the supporter, since peer support is a human skill that has to be practiced, not automated.

Applied the AI Fluency 4D framework (Delegation, Description, Discernment, Diligence): scoped what to hand to Claude versus build directly, refined prompts through an iterative description-discernment loop, and reviewed every generated output against the spec before shipping.

Designed a six-scenario training curriculum, including two crisis-escalation drills, with safety constraints in the persona system prompt that block any means/method content even under direct questioning.

Implemented a two-pass LLM architecture: a streaming roleplay engine (SSE) plus a separate structured-JSON evaluator that scores trainees against a support rubric with quoted evidence and coaching suggestions.
Used Anthropic prompt caching to keep per-turn cost low as conversations grow, with a configurable session cap as a spend guardrail.
Designed a custom visual identity — typography, color tokens, and a signature 'presence ring' motif — prioritizing calm, non-clinical UX over conventional dashboard/chat patterns.
